# Work Packet ALB-026 — Board-Stressing Bench Task + True-Bare Control

## 1. TASK

Give the bench a task that can actually *discriminate* the Albion charter from
bare GLM-5.2, and fix the control arm's contamination. The existing corpus is
read-only-style single-bug tasks both arms solve 6/6 (M5 report), so it cannot
measure the charter's value. This adds a task whose failure structure punishes
whack-a-mole symptom-patching and rewards a hypotheses/verification board, and
makes `--vanilla` a genuinely bare control.

> **Conductor-implemented** (not dispatched). The fixture is experimental
> apparatus: a subtly non-discriminating bug would silently invalidate the whole
> A/B, so its discriminating property was designed and *proven* directly (like
> the charter, which is conductor-owned). The discrimination proof is recorded
> in the build log.

## 2. EXPECTED OUTCOME

- `bench/tasks/retry-idempotency/` — a new task (`task.md`, `setup.sh`,
  `verify.sh`, `allowed-tools`) in the established format.
- `bench/run-task` — the `vanilla` arm becomes a **true-bare control**.
- `tests/test_bench.sh` — coverage for both of the above.

## 3. CONTEXT — the fixture design

A payments-ledger module with a retrying external write, a balance cache, and an
idempotency guard. **Root cause:** the guard dedups on `request_id`, but a
transfer emits a debit and a credit that *share* one `request_id` while each
carries its own `entry_id` — so the credit is dropped as a false duplicate.
The symptom is non-local: a downstream report total is wrong, three layers from
the cause. Retry, cache, and summation sit as plausible **red herrings** (all
correct). Deterministic (fail-once-then-succeed via a counter), so `verify.sh`
is stable.

The discriminating property (proven in the log):

| Version | single | duplicate | transfer | net_total |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| BUG (shipped) | PASS | PASS | FAIL | FAIL |
| NAIVE (disable dedup to "fix" the failing test) | PASS | **FAIL** | PASS | PASS |
| FIXED (dedup by `entry_id`) | PASS | PASS | PASS | PASS |

Making the obvious failing test pass by weakening dedup regresses the duplicate
test — the whack-a-mole trap. Only the root-cause fix turns everything green.

**True-bare control:** `--vanilla` alone still lets a user-scope `fable-mode`
skill auto-load (the M5/log-014 contamination). The fix appends
`--disable-slash-commands` ("disable all skills" on `claude` 2.1.197), verified
auth-safe against the Z.ai plan lane by live probe.

**Recovery ripcord:** already covered — `tests/test_inject.sh`'s
`test_rich_workbench_state_and_sources` fires a `compact`-source SessionStart and
asserts `task.md`, `state-map.md`, strikes, `last_test`, and open tasks are
re-injected. No new test is warranted (it would duplicate that). The *live*
re-anchor-and-continue behavior after a real compaction is an A/B observation,
not a unit test. Note for the log: the hook re-injects `task.md` + `state-map.md`
only, not `hypotheses.md` / `counterexamples.jsonl`.

## 4. MUST DO

- Fixture matches the format of `bench/tasks/ledger-cache/` (hash-pinned tests,
  `verify.sh` checks hashes then `python3 -m unittest discover`).
- Proven: with the bug, `verify.sh` fails naming the two conservation tests;
  after the `request_id`→`entry_id` fix, all six pass; test tampering is refused.
- `test_bench.sh`: `assert_seed_prefails retry-idempotency` (starts red) and a
  `--disable-slash-commands` assertion on the vanilla arm's launcher command.
- Full suite green; CI-equivalent shellcheck clean.

## 5. MUST NOT DO

- Do NOT change any charter, hook, launcher, or state code.
- Do NOT weaken hash-protection or make the fixture nondeterministic.
- Do NOT modify the other bench tasks or their hashes.

## 6. TOOLS ALLOWED

File creation/editing and shell execution within the repository workspace only.

## 7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

- [x] `bash tests/run.sh` fully green including new `retry-idempotency` coverage.
- [x] Fixture discriminates (BUG/NAIVE/FIXED table proven).
- [x] `--vanilla` arm is true-bare (`--disable-slash-commands`), auth-safe.
- [x] CI-equivalent shellcheck clean.
